definition: A yield curve shape with positive 2s10s but flat 3m10y, observed only three times since 1960 (1967-68, 2003-04, and now). The kink reflects different drivers at different maturities: front end anchored by Fed expectations, long end driven by fiscal term premium. Informative as transition zone diagnostic but not directionally deterministic, as prior episodes resolved in opposite directions.
evidence: Grounded in bond math not historical extrapolation (pair_3 assessment)
1967-68 resolved toward fiscal dominance; 2003-04 back to monetary dominance
'Agent A's strongest original contribution' per pair_3 debate
open_questions: What additional signals could resolve directional ambiguity of the kinked curve?
Could the kinked curve persist as a new structural norm under partial fiscal dominance?
Does the current instance more closely resemble 1967-68 or 2003-04 conditions?
